programme.desc: James is intrigued after meeting a cowhand, and Mr Dawson disturbs Siegfried. Rural drama about a North Yorkshire vet, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Brian Glover

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Richard employs a new farm manager to improve efficiency on the estate, and Audrey is forced to learn the art of housekeeping when Brabinger falls ill. Comedy, starring Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: The staff start feeling the cold thanks to Grace Brothers' latest plans to cut costs, and it's not long before they are airing their grievances in typical fashion. Classic comedy, starring Mollie Sugden and John Inman

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Barry is shocked to discover his golf captain has moved in next door when Glenda shows a keen interest in her new neighbours. Hobbo tries to help a dejected Morton feel less lonely, while Hobbo's driving skills attract unwanted attention when he takes to the wheel of a truck following a visit to an inn

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Hobbo discovers the whereabouts of his real mother and decides to pay her a visit, but is unimpressed by the welcome he receives. Marina chains herself to a supermarket trolley in protest against the unreliability of men, while Nelly reveals some information that greatly lifts Hobbo's spirits

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: The maverick High Court Judge's daughter is arrested during a demonstration, presenting him with the challenge of getting her off the hook without falling foul of powerful enemies he has made in his single-minded push to the top of the legal profession. Martin Shaw, Donald Sinden and Caroline Langrishe star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Brian struggles to control his urge to drink and finds refuge in a commune at the centre of the team's investigation into the death of a university student. The case also helps Gerry to face up to his relationship with Emily - who has not spoken to him since she discovered the truth about the DNA test at Ricky Hanson's trial. Police drama, starring Alun Armstrong and Amanda Redman

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Thirteen years after the kidnap and murder of a teenage girl, Boyd finds a crucial piece of evidence and arrests the owner - reclusive hippie Alec Garvey. As the pressure for a conviction mounts from the Met Commissioner and the media, Boyd charges his man - but after the suspect resorts to desperate measures, the detective begins to realise the case is much more complicated than he imagined. Trevor Eve stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Darren Day guest stars as a photographer arrested on suspicion of murdering one of his models. The discovery of a blood-soaked T-shirt at his trashed studio, and the evidence of his own pictures, suggests a second suspect may have delivered the fatal blows to the victim's head. Emma and Beth tangle with a trio of truculent young women whose troubled history of deceit and blackmail leads to violence, putting the young PCs in danger

programme.desc: Eleanor comes up against the governors of the East London Hospital when they threaten to take over the Thrift. Period drama, starring Jemma Redgrave

programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a woman in North Yorkshire who has £500,000 to find a country property back in her home county. During her time there, Margherita hits the production line at an organic farm which specialises in seasonal fruit-and-veg boxes

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two sets of farmers - David and George, and Liam and Alice - hope to make a splash with their premium ciders and harvested vegetables at a market in Ludlow

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers hosts as five dealers bid against each other for items brought in by the public, including an unusual Asian drum, an Argentinian belt and charming push-a-long toy

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio, presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents a programme in which five dealers bid against one another to buy intriguing items brought in by members of the public. The second episode features wannabe dealer Alfie, aged just 11, as he tries to sell his scooter to the dealers, and an unusual birthing chair and a pair of vintage phones come the dealers' way

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Bike restorer Tim Gunn brings all his skill to bear on a wartime bicycle once owned and used by a Bletchley Park codebreaker. Horologist Steve Fletcher recruits fellow expert and sister Suzie to work on a neglected and battered Victorian firefighter's helmet. Woodworker Will Kirk and silversmith Brenton West team up to repair a 19th-century stereoscope viewer that sparked a lifelong obsession, and soft toy rest toy restorers Amanda Middleditch and Julie Tatchell try to make a faceless old teddy bear smile again

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: With millions of people swapping the gym for the living room during the Covid-19 pandemic, Mehreen Baig delves into the latest science to find the best way to get fit at home. She uncovers exciting new research testing the theory that just one minute of strength training per week on each main muscle group might reduce your risk of diabetes and heart disease, and also looks at the impact wearable fitness tech actually has on our willpower to stick to an exercise plan. Later, she investigates whether popular supplements like pre-trainers and protein shakes really work

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, which included Aston Villa v Tottenham Hotspur and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Everton. Plus, highlights of Sheffield United v Newcastle United and Manchester City v Brighton & Hove Albion

programme.title: Fight for First: Excel Esports
programme.desc: Documentary following Excel Esports owner Kieran Holmes-Darby and his team as they fight to be taken seriously in the global video games scene

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former Premier League midfielder Steve Sidwell, Winter Olympic snowboarder Amy Fuller, world indoor champion sprinter Richard Kitty and netball star Pamela Cookey join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: After losing her father, Cheryl Castling was determined to live life to the fullest, and thought that a move to Oz could be the adventure she and her husband Adam needed. Adam was less than convinced, despite being the one to have the idea in the first place. Could a week of living the Aussie life make him see where Cheryl was coming from? Four years on, have they been able to come to a decision about where home should be? Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Husband-and-wife cider-makers Liz and Mike are excited to bring their cider, made from eight varieties of apples, to the South of England Agricultural Show. Meanwhile, Andy, his wife Anna and their two young children Lillie and Daisy have spent the last few days gearing up for the show's poultry competition. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a sock factory in Leicester that produces one and a half million socks annually, while Cherry Healey discovers the science behind smelly feet and tests brands of socks to see how they cope with the problem. Ruth Goodman looks back at how socks became a fashion statement in the 1980s and a vital piece of military kit in the First World War

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Planets
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox tells the life story of the solar system, beginning with the development of the four rocky worlds closest to the Sun - Mercury, Venus, Earth and Mars. They were born at the same time from the same material - yet have lived radically different lives. What immense forces are at play?

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Stuart Bingham v Thepchaiya Un-Nooh. Coverage of the first-round match on day two at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, where Bingham gets the defence of his title under way. The former world champion faces an intriguing challenge against his Thai opponent, who is regarded as the quickest player on the tour and competing in the Masters for the first time in his career

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Interesting finds from the show's travels around the country, including a late-19th-century Steiff cow on rollers and a joystick from a World War One aeroplane

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: David Baddiel, Alex Brooker, Sophie Duker and Charlotte Hawkins go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick journeys inland from the majestic Camel Estuary to one of Britain's finest vineyards, where he joins in harvesting the grapes to discover that the Cornish weather is perfect making some of the best wine in the country. Just off Pentire Point, as the sun sets, Rick rides the gentle swell of the north Atlantic with an old friend, casting his line to hook Britain's fastest fish, before firing up the barbeque to cook his catch with a recipe inspired from a trip to Goa. Rick joins Tim Smit, best known for starting the hugely successful Eden Project, who reveals to Rick his new and ambitious idea in Cornwall

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Mark Chapman introduces coverage of the FA Cup fourth-round draw, as the remaining sides start to look towards Wembley Stadium and their potential route to the latter stages

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Four more contenders take to the black chair under the spotlight and the ticking clock to answer questions on their Specialist Subjects and general knowledge. John Humphrys asks the questions, which tonight are on The Fall and Rise of Reginald Perrin, Marlene Dumas, the life and poetry of Edward Thomas and Guy Burgess

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: A team of murder mystery enthusiasts take on three Beatles fans for a last chance to make the quarter-finals. They'll compete to draw together the connections between things which, at first glance, seem utterly random. Victoria Coren Mitchell presents the quiz show

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions in the first of the quarter-finals, with two teams competing to reach the next stage of the competition

programme.title: Death in Bollywood
programme.desc: Documentary following the British family of actress Jiah Khan in a search for the truth about her death in Mumbai in 2013, which was ruled a suicide by police. Jiah's mother Rabbiya hired private forensic investigator Jason Payne James, who believes it is highly unlikely that a distinctive oval mark found on the 25-year-old's neck could be caused by the dupatta scarf she was found hanging from in her flat

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Doc Martin star Martin Clunes hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz from October 2019, with Layla Moran and Sara Pascoe joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at that week's events

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Snooker: The Masters - Extra
programme.desc: Stuart Bingham v Thepchaiya Un-Nooh and Shaun Murphy v Mark Williams. The key moments from day two at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, where the first round continued

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher looks back at 2020, a difficult year when the countryside became a lifeline for unprecedented numbers during the pandemic, while wildlife made their homes in curious places during lockdown. Sean also catches up with young people at the forefront of the battle to protect the environment

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce revisits some of the show's most exciting finds, and discovers what happened when the cameras stopped rolling. She reveals the outcome of the auction for a special guitar belonging to the Beatles and the mystery of the missing blueprint for the Royal Albert Hall, before going behind the scenes of Pinewood Film and TV Studios to learn about its wartime history, its famous costumes, and a giant golden gong

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: The stories of pivotal moments in police investigations when detectives realise they have cracked a case, told through interviews and dramatic reconstructions. In the first edition, a single text message - hidden among thousands - nails a serial fraudster, and an extraordinary surveillance operation leads straight to the kidnappers and their hostage

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: After a terrible accident turned their lives upside down, Stan and Andrea thought Australia was the answer. Six years on, Nikki Chapman finds out whether the dream become reality

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two families prepare for the farmers' market in historic Stokesley - rare-breed pig farmers Nigel and Benjamin, and the Mellanby family with their harvested vegetables

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In Armadale, at the northernmost tip of the Scottish mainland, Storm Ciara is wreaking havoc for hill farmer Joyce. Meanwhile, Father and daughter team Frank and Georgia Hunter are also battling the storm on their mixed livestock farm in Cumbria, and when the storm ruptures a section of the mains water pipe, their water supply is cut off. Elsewhere, Helen and Graham deal with a missing calf

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Planets
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox continues his tour of the solar system revealing that it was once home to not one, but two blue planets. Today, Mars is an arid desert world that long ago had the same potential as Earth. Which begs a big question - did life get a start on both worlds?

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Neil Robertson v Yan Bingtao. Day three at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes gets under way with another first-round match, with Robertson starting as favourite to progress to the quarter-finals. The Thunder from Down Under has been one of the form players in recent months and won an epic battle against world number one Judd Trump in the final of the UK Championship in December

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Ragley Hall, Warwickshire, antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Charlie Ross find a selection of antiques and collectibles to take to auction. Christina gets to grips with a weighty jardiniere, Charlie tests out a horse-measuring stick on its owner and presenter Paul Martin steps into the world of folk art

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: David Baddiel, Alex Brooker, Sophie Duker and Charlotte Hawkins go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the Fowey Estuary, one of Cornwall's hidden secrets, joining a friend on a boat trip to learn about the history of the area and getting a unique view of the buildings along the riverbank. He discovers the area's history of piracy, meets a family that make award-winning vodka, and makes a pancake with a wild blackberry compote and Cornish clotted cream

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: The largest boats in the fishing fleet head out to sea in autumn, including giant netters and trawlers up to 100 feet long capable of fishing hundreds of miles from shore. On one boat, an engineer and deckhand with 10 years of service behind him is training to become a captain, under the watchful eye of his veteran skipper. The vessel faces a rough week at sea in gale force winds. Back on land, the owner of a shellfish company that employs workers from Latvia and Lithuania fears for the future as Britain leaves the EU

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a factory in rural Somerset that produces one million pots of yoghurt every 24 hours, and meets the herd of cows that produce the milk. Cherry Healey helps out with the UK's biggest blackcurrant harvest and tries out plant-based alternatives to milk, while historian Ruth Goodman reveals the stories behind the electric milk float and the cream tea

programme.title: Death in Bollywood
programme.desc: Jiah Khan' family believe that was murdered, and her actor boyfriend Sooraj Pancholi is their prime suspect. In this instalment of the documentary, Pancholi tells his side of the story, with the actor facing charges of abetment of suicide as police believed he had driven her to take her own life

programme.title: Elis James: Funny Nation
programme.desc: The stand-up explores the history of Welsh comedy on radio, film and TV output, and looks back to a time when millions tuned in to a wartime radio show that was a platform for countless Welsh stars. He discusses comedy films that defined their eras, as well as much-loved sitcoms and sketch shows with their creators and stars.

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: Lola has not had a customer in her beauty salon for two days so Mandy suggests she introduce fish pedicures. Only to buy the wrong kind of fish. Sean Lock guest stars in the comedy, with Diane Morgan and Michelle Greenidge

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories. With Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Mark Selby v Stephen Maguire. Action from the sixth first-round match at at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, where a place in the quarter-finals was at stake

programme.title: Snooker: The Masters - Extra
programme.desc: Neil Robertson v Yan Bingtao and Mark Selby v Stephen Maguire. Action from the first-round matches on day three at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes

programme.title: Last Woman on Earth with Sara Pascoe
programme.desc: The comedian's world tour of unusual jobs takes her to Georgia, a country bordered by Russia and the Black Sea that has gone through a drastic period of modernisation in the last 10 years - leaving behind some very rare trades with centuries of history. Among the last practitioners of distinctive Georgian skills are a gunsmith, a polyphonic singer and a guide at the Stalin Museum

programme.title: Being Bridget Jones
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating 25 years of Helen Fielding's hapless creation. The programme examines the ways in which society's attitudes to women have shifted in 25 years, along with the ways in which female writers have told their own stories since Bridget made her debut. Featuring interviews with Helen Fielding, and the friends she based the original characters on, as well as tributes from celebrity fans

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A pair of pyjamas reveal the shocking true identity of an attacker who left his victim for dead, and a bright yellow baseball cap puts a daylight robber behind bars

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with Stef Barker, who had been trying to convince her husband to move to New Zealand with their family

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two farming families gear up for the horse carriage marathon at the Ashfield's Carriage and Polo Club in Essex, including a past national competitor in the junior championships

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Nadeem Islam meets Jean Pentland, who takes him on a tour of the Scottish Highlands, where he samples local cuisine, history and culture. With voiceover

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson shares recipes that can be made in advance for cheesecake ice-cream and a fish stew, as well as a fried chicken sandwich garnished with some of her favourite condiments. Nigella also makes a simple yet daring dish of cherry flambé using frozen fruit and kirsch, and uses up the final slices of a soft white loaf to prepare rum-spiked French toast

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Planets
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox examines Jupiter, the largest and oldest planet in the solar system, revealing how its immense gravity influenced where other planets developed, destroying several nascent planets and negatively influencing the development of Mars. Today, Jupiter's great mass continues to be felt. Its gravity torments its moon Io, creating the most volcanically active body in the solar system. And through the vice-like grip it exerts over the asteroid belt, Jupiter's influence extends as far as Earth

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Ronnie O'Sullivan v Ding Junhui. Day four at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes gets under way with the seventh first-round match, with a quarter-final against John Higgins or Mark Allen awaiting the winner. This is the fifth time the pair have met in the Masters and the Rocket has been victorious on each occasion, and has lifted the trophy a record seven times

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Adam Partridge value items brought to Muncaster Castle, near Ravenglass in Cumbria, while Paul Martin finds out about the Lake District's bobbin industry

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: David Baddiel, Alex Brooker, Sophie Duker and Charlotte Hawkins go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: On the wild north Atlantic coast, Rick heads out to sea to go fishing for lobsters with his old friend and celebrity chef Nathan Outlaw, before helping to cook up a lobster risotto. Later, at Tresillian House near Newquay, Rick meets with head gardener John Harris to find out more about his Victorian walled garden

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: Britain's departure from the EU brings changes for Newlyn, Cornwall's biggest fishing port. The harbourmaster oversees a redevelopment of the fish market, as well as making plans to transform the harbour's ageing infrastructure. However, many fishermen resent his grand plans, feeling that they were not consulted over massive changes to their working environment. While many fishermen supported leaving the EU to gain control over quotas, many now fear that their industry will be sacrificed during negotiations

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Hill farmer Joyce Campbell prepares spring, and does everything she can to make sure her 800 North Country Cheviot ewes are healthy ahead of birthing season. Meanwhile in the Lake District, Matt and Dani Blair are preparing for the first cattle TB test on their new farm, and in Aberdeenshire, spring has come early for new entrant farmers Isla and George at Little Rowater Farm

programme.title: Death in Bollywood
programme.desc: After two new enquiries conclude that Jiah Khan had died by suicide, the actress's family commission a legal case review from a British law firm, which finds there were faults with the police investigations from the night she died onwards and that vital evidence may have been missed

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1987
programme.desc: Some of 1987's biggest music stars look back on the year, which saw Margaret Thatcher secure a third term as prime minister, while the Channel Tunnel began construction and Britain was battered by the Great Storm. While those events unfolded in the outside world, the TOTP studio provided a haven for those who wanted to escape. Stock, Aitken and Waterman provided the floor-fillers as hip-hop and rap made their mark on the chart alongside the homegrown House genre. Contributors include Terence Trent D'Arby, Curiosity Killed the Cat, Rick Astley, Kim Appleby, Andy Bell, Carol Decker and Belinda Carlisle

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: John Higgins v Mark Allen. Action from the concluding first-round match at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, between the Wizard of Wishaw and the Pistol

programme.title: Snooker: The Masters - Extra
programme.desc: Ronnie O'Sullivan v Ding Junhui and John Higgins v Mark Allen. A look back at the key moments from day four at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Nadeem Islam meets Jean Pentland, who takes him on a tour of the Scottish Highlands, where he samples local cuisine, history and culture. With voiceover

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: A young meerkat queen lives in the harsh beauty of the Madgadikgadi salt pans. If her new pups are to survive, she must unite her family in the face of rivals, predators and immense dust storms. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a discarded scrap of paper found in a household bin helped convict a killer, and a distinctive mole holds the key to tracking down a dangerous and predatory attacker

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Shaun Murphy v Mark Williams. Coverage of the first-round match at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, as two former world champions look to progress to the quarter-finals

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The critic returns to examines more of the techniques and conventions behind classic movie genres, beginning by looking at the tropes of British comedies. Mark argues that they win laughs by tapping into the public's abiding national preoccupations, from rooting for the underdog to the subversion of class and manners, as well as the audience's fascination with comedy and crime, and pastiches and parodies that take the big beasts of Hollywood down a peg or two

programme.title: The Night Manager
programme.desc: Apostol throws a lavish party to celebrate his daughter's birthday - but when the guest of honour is found dead, he becomes vulnerable and Burr is able to gain leverage over him. Roper digs deeper into Pine's fictitious criminal past and offers him a job in his organisation - but Corky remains suspicious of the newcomer. When Pine is given permission to take Daniel into town, he and Burr have a chance to share intelligence and deal with a potential threat to their plans by persuading the troubled Apostol to turn on Corky

programme.title: The Night Manager
programme.desc: Pine is now firmly embedded in Roper's organisation and gets involved in an arms deal, but his relationship with Jed, and Corky's growing jealousy, jeopardise his cover. Burr puts pressure on Apostol to help her translate the information she has received on Roper's offshore operation, but begins to suspect the arms dealer has been buying weapons from the British government - a fear that Palfrey confirms. A tip-off leads her to Istanbul, but her attempt to catch Roper in the act puts Pine in further danger

programme.title: Victorian Sensations
programme.desc: Mathematician Dr Hannah Fry is joined by actor Paul McGann and psychotherapist Philippa Perry to explore the technology, art and culture of the last years of Queen Victoria's reign. In the first episode, Hannah investigates how innovations in science and technology - including the X-ray, safety bicycle and proto-aeroplane - transformed Britain in the 1890s, with the end of the 19th century marking the beginning of a new 'Electric Age'

programme.title: Handmade in Mexico
programme.desc: Documentary about traditional Mexican crafts and those preserving them, beginning by following the making of a loose-fitting tunic known as a huipil

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The critic returns to examines more of the techniques and conventions behind classic movie genres, beginning by looking at the tropes of British comedies. Mark argues that they win laughs by tapping into the public's abiding national preoccupations, from rooting for the underdog to the subversion of class and manners, as well as the audience's fascination with comedy and crime, and pastiches and parodies that take the big beasts of Hollywood down a peg or two

programme.title: Inside Obama's White House
programme.desc: Four-part series giving an account of Barack Obama's successes and failures, as told by his inner circle and chief adversaries, including the story of how he tried to reshape America. The election of the first African American President marked an era of hope, but what happened instead was economic collapse, minimal cooperation from the Republican opposition, and the rise of a populist anti-big government Tea Party. The first edition focuses on how Obama crashed a private meeting of fellow heads of state to persuade the Chinese to tackle climate change at the Copenhagen summit, as well as his struggle to shut down Guantanamo

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Mark Selby v Stephen Maguire. Coverage of the sixth first-round match at at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, where a place in the quarter-finals is at stake

programme.title: Inside Obama's White House
programme.desc: The story of how Barack Obama resolved to reform the healthcare system in the United States, despite stern opposition from the Republicans and within his own party. The Affordable Care Act, also known as Obamacare, was finally signed into law in 2010 after years of wrangling, as senior Democrats urged Obama to be less ambitious with his plans and Tea Party protesters gained greater prominence. While the President eventually got the act passed, the consequences have been felt for the rest of his tenure, with the Republicans blocking all future major reforms after securing huge gains in the mid-term elections

programme.title: The Night Manager
programme.desc: Roper discovers that crucial documents have been leaked and becomes suspicious that he has a traitor in his team. Pine is asked to demonstrate weapons to one of the arms dealer's clients, and is confronted with the full horror of Roper's actions. Dromgoole tries to persuade Burr to abandon her investigation, leaving her afraid for her family's safety

programme.title: The Night Manager
programme.desc: Roper's final deal with Barghati takes place in Cairo, and Pine is forced to return to the Nefertiti Hotel, where he first became aware of the arms dealer. This leads him to unexpectedly bump into the owner, jeopardising his cover story. Burr faces the consequences of her failed mission to intercept one of Roper's arms shipments, and suspects Pine has turned against her. Jed risks her life to obtain the combination to a safe containing vital documents - but has to do so under the watchful gaze of Caroline

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Gilou is offered a way out of jail if he agrees to become a police informer, but on the condition that he keeps away from Laure. Meanwhile, the police team focus their investigation on the Paris neighbourhood of Barbès, where vulnerable delinquent minors are being exploited by receivers of stolen goods. In French

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: The police investigation screeches to a sudden halt when Souleymane disappears. How and where will they find him? Meanwhile, Laure flouts the ban on seeing Gilou, who has got himself hired as a bouncer in Cisco's nightclub. There he meets the security manager, Cisco's son Titi. In French

programme.title: Museums in Quarantine
programme.desc: In a series exploring national collections at a time of enforced closure due to Covid-19, Alastair Sooke examines Tate Modern's Andy Warhol exhibition. Covering works from across the American artist's career, he explores Warhol's long-running commitment to experimental film and TV, as well as his fascination with advertising, pop music and commerce

programme.title: Inside Obama's White House
programme.desc: The story of how Barack Obama resolved to reform the healthcare system in the United States, despite stern opposition from the Republicans and within his own party. The Affordable Care Act, also known as Obamacare, was finally signed into law in 2010 after years of wrangling, as senior Democrats urged Obama to be less ambitious with his plans and Tea Party protesters gained greater prominence. While the President eventually got the act passed, the consequences have been felt for the rest of his tenure, with the Republicans blocking all future major reforms after securing huge gains in the mid-term elections

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: John Higgins v Mark Allen. Coverage of the concluding first-round match at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, between the Wizard of Wishaw and the Pistol

programme.title: Mrs Brown (1997)
programme.desc: Queen Victoria is helped by a no-nonsense Scottish manservant to overcome her long period of mourning for the death of her husband. However, it is not long before their subsequent friendship is frowned upon by members of the court. John Madden's period drama, starring Judi Dench, Billy Connolly, Geoffrey Palmer, Antony Sher and Gerard Butler

programme.title: Timewatch
programme.desc: Kate Williams explores the story of the monarch who brought Great Britain into the modern age. She examines Victoria's battle to become Queen, in which she fought against her mother, the Duchess of Kent, who wanted to ascend the throne herself - all at a time when the country was on the brink of revolution

programme.title: Queen Victoria: My Musical Britain
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley explores the character and legacy of the famous monarch through music, considering how Victoria used music to bring the country together during a time of great upheaval and change. Lucy also examines the role music played in the monarch's own life - as a queen, a private person and in her marriage - and how Victoria and Albert established institutions like the Royal College of Music and the Royal Albert Hall. The documentary features performances by Willard White, the BBC Symphony Orchestra and Black Dyke Brass Band

programme.title: Forest, Field & Sky: Art Out of Nature
programme.desc: James Fox journeys through six different landscapes across Britain, meeting artists whose work explores the human relationship with the natural world, from Andy Goldsworthy's stone sculptures to James Turrell's extraordinary Skyspaces

programme.title: Timewatch

Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy go together and have a great time, but Bad Idea Puppy has to go on his own and struggles to slide down. After the slide, they visit the Frosty Frolics Fayre, and Good Idea Puppy and Love Monster accidentally choose activities made for two all day long. Finally, just as Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy are about to go on a tandem ski through the forest, Bad Idea Puppy asks if this time he can go with his cousin so he won't be the odd puppy out. Love Monster happily agrees, but soon he realises that frosty frolics aren't fun on your own, and that Bad Idea Puppy must have been feeling left out all day.

programme.title: Bing

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The children make heads out of egg shells and cress seeds

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The squirrels have had an exhausting morning playing. After their milk they can have their nap. However, Tag cannot sleep, so Duggee tries to help with his Sleeping Badge. He tells Tag about how other animals sleep and a few tricks they use to manage it

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: On a stormy day, Duggee makes banana muffins. Suddenly there is a flash of white light and it turns out the squirrels are scared of lightning

programme.title: Octonauts

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: T builds a tall tower of teacups, declining the assistance of accident-prone A. Fun and games with the 26 letters which come to life, tell stories and make words

programme.title: Justin's House

programme.title: Raa Raa the Noisy Lion

programme.title: Yakka Dee!

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Pug wants to write exciting stories and our two friends meet Enid Brighton. She has a very vivid imagination and they go on an adventure to solve all of her mysteries. Luckily, it is all in her imagination and they return safely to the bookshop.

programme.title: Dipdap
programme.desc: The Line draws a sky full of stars, but Dipdap accidentally knocks one of them down

programme.title: Teletubbies

programme.title: The Toddler Club at Home
programme.desc: Ideas of things to do with your toddler based on what you can find outside. Hosted by Giovanna from her own home. See how spotting and pointing at things can be a great way to discover the outdoors together. Join in with the rhyme Incy Wincy Spider!

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Today we meet a new family, five-year-old twins Daisy and Lily. They live near the seaside with their mum, dad and younger twin sisters Molly and Nell. The family has a busy breakfast time with all those mouths to feed, and Daisy and Lily are in charge of counting the eggs. Later, Mum takes the girls out for some fun on the beach where they make a picture using pebbles and seaweed. Back at home Daisy and Lily get to work in their toy kitchen, making pretend soup for all the family. But will their sausage roll and vegetable soup be a success at their super-soup party?

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: The little girl gets upset when she does not win at a game, so her pet spider shows her the importance of having fun just by taking part

programme.desc: Lexi and her family are from Wales and she is cooking Glamorgan sausages using two very special Welsh ingredients

programme.title: Timmy Time

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales

programme.title: Numberblocks

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing is playing the talking lettuce game on Flop's phone when he drops the phone and accidentally breaks it. Too panicked to tell Flop what he's done, Bing puts the phone in the bin. However, he's so upset that Flop knows something has happened

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: Messy explores science questions that children ask about the world around us. When a little turtle gets stranded on the beach in OKIDO, Messy helps her rejoin her friends so they can begin their very important journey across the ocean.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friend paint a pot, and Artiste Tumble paints a portrait

programme.title: Teletubbies

programme.title: Mister Maker at Home
programme.desc: Mister Maker is excited about singing with his friend Evie, and so he video-calls her. This leads to an arty idea that might help them both sing like stars! Mister Maker makes a pretend microphone out of an old sponge ball and a kitchen roll tube. Later, Mister Maker checks in on his friends, The Shapes. They are snoring away, so Mister Maker makes sure he leaves them to have a nice rest. Mister Maker then decides to dance like a Shape himself, which in turn inspires him to make some shape art. Mister Maker shows how to make a stand up version of his friend Triangle out of a square of paper. Lastly, Mister Maker remembers all the fun he has had throughout the day.

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: Ferne Corrigan joins a farm vet visiting some cows, and Rory Crawford meets some babies getting a vet check

programme.title: Our Family

programme.title: Swashbuckle

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: There is an amazing seahorse merry-go-round on the beach, and everyone is having lots of fun going round and round - except Bevel. When he runs away from the ride, Bob finds him, only for Bevel to admit he's nervous about going in circles. Everyone joins in to help him overcome his fear and soon he's sitting on his seahorse, Blue Yah, excited about his first turn on a merry-go-round. But now the key that winds it up has broken, so Bitz needs to work out how to get it started using wind power

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Bike restorer Tim Gunn brings all his skill to bear on a wartime bicycle once owned and used by a Bletchley Park codebreaker. Horologist Steve Fletcher recruits fellow expert and sister Suzie to work on a neglected and battered Victorian firefighter's helmet. Woodworker Will Kirk and silversmith Brenton West team up to repair a 19th-century stereoscope viewer that sparked a lifelong obsession, and soft toy rest toy restorers Amanda Middleditch and Julie Tatchell try to make a faceless old teddy bear smile again

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: With millions of people swapping the gym for the living room during the Covid-19 pandemic, Mehreen Baig delves into the latest science to find the best way to get fit at home. She uncovers exciting new research testing the theory that just one minute of strength training per week on each main muscle group might reduce your risk of diabetes and heart disease, and also looks at the impact wearable fitness tech actually has on our willpower to stick to an exercise plan. Later, she investigates whether popular supplements like pre-trainers and protein shakes really work

programme.title: Scot Squad
programme.desc: The Chief is keen to make amends for a slip of the tongue and keeps digging deeper as his extended apology lasts for ever. The traffic cops make a big noise to salute the key workers, while the rural officers handle a massive marrow bust-up at the horticultural show. Comedy, starring Jack Docherty, Grado and Karen Bartke

programme.title: Martin Clunes: Islands of Australia
programme.desc: With more than 8,000 islands to choose from, Martin Clunes visits 16 which provide an insight into the diversity, history and challenges of island life in Australia. His first stop is Lord Howe Island, where the pace of life is slow and the population is a steady 350. He also visits Norfolk Island, Australia's most easterly territory, and the remotest island on Martin's journey

programme.title: The Pembrokeshire Murders
programme.desc: With the second parole hearing looming closer and still no conclusive results from forensic testing, Wilkins and his team face Cooper head-on in three days of interviews. Part two of the three-part crime drama, starring Luke Evans and Keith Allen

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Storming the Capitol: The Inside Story
programme.desc: In shocking and unbelievable scenes, the very heart of the United States government was stormed by a crowd of Trump supporters on Wednesday afternoon, just as legislators were formalising the election of President Trump's successor, Joseph R. Biden. This assault on the symbol of American democracy was perhaps the inevitable outcome of the Trump era, where conspiracy theory and lies were fed to the people by the President himself. ITV's Robert Moore and his team were the only network TV news crew to enter the building with the aggrieved and infuriated rioters that infamous day - and they obtained unique and exclusive footage of what went on inside. As America reels in shock at these events, and legislators contemplate whether to remove Trump from office, Robert Moore talks us through the unprecedented events of that day with, as yet, unseen footage from his time inside the Capitol in an afternoon that became the defining and dramatic conclusion to President Trump's turbulent time in off

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: David Walliams presents a further compilation of TV clangers, gaffes and fluffed cues, all rescued from the cutting-room floor. Clips include Olly Murs being attacked by a fly on The Voice, a goat running amok in Judge Rinder's court, a Love islander discovering the danger of wearing high heels on TV, and a desperate dog relieving himself on Paul O'Grady

He also visits Norfolk Island, Australia's most easterly territory, and the remotest island on Martin's journey

programme.title: The Pembrokeshire Murders

programme.title: Storming the Capitol: The Inside Story
programme.desc: In shocking and unbelievable scenes, the very heart of the United States government was stormed by a crowd of Trump supporters on Wednesday afternoon, just as legislators were formalising the election of President Trump's successor, Joseph R. Biden. This assault on the symbol of American democracy was perhaps the inevitable outcome of the Trump era, where conspiracy theory and lies were fed to the people by the President himself. ITV's Robert Moore and his team were the only network TV news crew to enter the building with the aggrieved and infuriated rioters that infamous day - and they obtained unique and exclusive footage of what went on inside. As America reels in shock at these events, and legislators contemplate whether to remove Trump from office, Robert Moore talks us through the unprecedented events of that day with, as yet, unseen footage from his time inside the Capitol in an afternoon that became the defining and dramatic conclusion to President Trump's turbulent time in off

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: David Walliams presents a further compilation of TV clangers, gaffes and fluffed cues, all rescued from the cutting-room floor. programme.title: Classic Coronation Street

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin is stunned when Natalie tells him Sally slapped her. Percy starts to keep a record of the Battersbys' anti-social behaviour. Natalie pours out her heart to Audrey

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris isn't happy about the Dingles being at Home Farm. Zoe tells Kelly she is welcome to stay with her. Zak gets a shock when he opens the boot of Lisa's classic car

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Terry encourages Will to think more seriously about playing rugby. Butch and Zak argue about who is going to bury the body. Donna and Kirsty decide to get their noses pierced

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Anti-fur protester Sue Padgett finds herself in deep trouble when her boyfriend and fellow crusader Nick Payton starts a fire at a haulier's depot. Carol and Chris fall out when they offer opposing advice to a pregnant teenager, while Peggy enthusiastically prepares David for a date with a farmer's ample daughter Tina Bestwick. David Roper and Amie Titterton guest star

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid dismisses Jean's interest in the daily horoscopes as complete nonsense - but his opinion changes when they start coming true

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George seeks solace when his budgie dies, but Mildred is too preoccupied with finding something good enough to donate to the jumble sale. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster teams up with a dashing man to investigate a series of poison-pen letters in the sleepy village of Lymstock. Two of the missives have led to the deaths of Mona Symmington, the wife of Lymstock solicitor Richard, and Agnes Woddell, the parlour maid at the Symmingtons' house. However, Miss Marple suspects there is more to these deaths than meets the eye. Murder mystery with an all-star cast, featuring Geraldine McEwan, James D'Arcy, Emilia Fox, Imogen Stubbs, Ken Russell, Harry Enfield and Kelly Brook

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: When teenager Vivienne Middleton is reported missing Joe suspects her disappearance has something to do with her American boyfriend Bobby, and sets out to locate him. Meanwhile, David and Peggy's latest exploit, to use Greengrass's old metal detector to find artefacts about the village, puts them under suspicion when priceless bronze statues are stolen from local estates. Guest starring Adrian Lukis and Tessa Peake-Jones

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Butch falls asleep while guarding the body. Micky Bell gives Steve a hard time at the quarry. Chris scolds the Dingles for making a mess of the nursery flat

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim is annoyed at Steve's behaviour the previous night. The headmaster excludes Donna and Kirsty from school. A fire breaks out at the Hutchinsons' house

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Janice orders Leanne to find a job. Ashley is upset to hear about Maxine being with Steve. Alan accuses Fiona of still being in love with Steve and breaks their engagement

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly has a black eye and has to buy new glasses. Fiona puts a brave face on her bust-up with Alan. Natalie is thrilled when Kevin tells her he has decided he wants to be with her

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Troy is playing in an annual cricket match between Midsomer Worthy and Fletchers Cross when tragedy strikes. The wife of one of the team captains, a wealthy local landowner, is found battered to death with a cricket bat in a quarry. As Barnaby and Troy investigate, they discover no end of people with a grudge against the victim's husband - who may have been complicit in some other suspicious deaths. Guest starring Imelda Staunton and Robert Hardy

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth takes a break on the island of Rhodes, where an English beauty draws attention by flirting with fellow guests at the Palace Hotel. When she is found murdered, her husband is immediately suspected, but Poirot quickly deduces there is more to the case than a simple crime of passion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: When a wealthy businessman is killed in a mysterious explosion aboard his fishing boat and suspicion falls on his son, Wycliffe feels sure the real culprit is to be found elsewhere and unearths several locals with a motive for murder. Jack Shepherd stars as the Cornish copper, with Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Seventy-year-old Susan Webb is desperate find the daughter she gave up for adoption in 1968. At the time, she was suffering with depression, and after being treated with electroconvulsive therapy that severely affected her memory, she was left with no recollections of her child. Meanwhile, Sonia Walsh looks for the mother that abandoned her at the age of three with her younger sister. During her investigations, she discovered her mother had also given up another baby, and she is now determined to make up for the pain of the past. Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: First episode of the drama, starring Michael Kitchen as a jaded police superintendent who would rather be fighting the enemy in France than crime on the south coast as the Second World War gathers pace in 1940. However, when a local magistrate's German wife is murdered amid a growing cloud of uncertainty and anti-Nazi feeling, the ensuing investigation is anything but routine. Edward Fox, Robert Hardy, David Horovitch, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks co-star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length drama. While holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London, Miss Marple is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers when the reading of a will leads to a double murder. Geraldine McEwan stars as the elderly spinster with a nose for a mystery, with guest appearances by Peter Davison, Mica Paris and Francesca Annis

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Janice orders Leanne to find a job. Ashley is upset to hear about Maxine being with Steve. Alan accuses Fiona of still being in love with Steve and breaks their engagement

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly has a black eye and has to buy new glasses. Fiona puts a brave face on her bust-up with Alan. Natalie is thrilled when Kevin tells her he has decided he wants to be with her

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Butch falls asleep while guarding the body. Micky Bell gives Steve a hard time at the quarry. Chris scolds the Dingles for making a mess of the nursery flat

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim is annoyed at Steve's behaviour the previous night. The headmaster excludes Donna and Kirsty from school. A fire breaks out at the Hutchinsons' house

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: When teenager Vivienne Middleton is reported missing Joe suspects her disappearance has something to do with her American boyfriend Bobby, and sets out to locate him. Meanwhile, David and Peggy's latest exploit, to use Greengrass's old metal detector to find artefacts about the village, puts them under suspicion when priceless bronze statues are stolen from local estates. Guest starring Adrian Lukis and Tessa Peake-Jones

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid permits Mike's dancer girlfriend to stay for a couple of days, but soon changes his mind when he finds out what sort of performer she really is. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George celebrates his 24th wedding anniversary by presenting Mildred with a carriage clock. However, he has purchased it cut-price from a dubious source, and when the Fourmiles return from holiday they find their home burgled, and among the missing items is a carriage clock. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: First episode of the drama, starring Michael Kitchen as a jaded police superintendent who would rather be fighting the enemy in France than crime on the south coast as the Second World War gathers pace in 1940. However, when a local magistrate's German wife is murdered amid a growing cloud of uncertainty and anti-Nazi feeling, the ensuing investigation is anything but routine. Edward Fox, Robert Hardy, David Horovitch, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks co-star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Joe and Carol join forces to unravel the mystery of a girl resident at Throckton Grange, when she collapses with exhaustion and expresses a fear of being taken away. Meanwhile, Geoff suffers from embarrassing bouts of sneezing when he tries to speak to Lucy who is temporarily working at the village shop, and David is surprised to be offered an unusual post at the local newspaper. Simon Shepherd and Victor McGuire guest star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Hutchinsons arrive at the Woolpack with their few remaining possessions. Chris tells the Dingles that he has found the body and is going to call the police

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tara is confused by Biff's cold behaviour towards her. Steve is ambushed by his fellow workers at the quarry. Terry takes Will to a rugby trial at Farsley Rugby Club

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin tells Natalie there is no hope of saving his marriage. Samantha is driven to distraction by the Battersbys. Gail is amazed when Roy gives her tickets to take Martin to Paris

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona is disgusted with herself for sleeping with Jim. Steve is amazed when Vicky's solicitor offers him a huge divorce settlement. Zoe has a shocking proposal for Judy and Gary

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Travellers descend on the sleepy village of Martyr Warren, antagonising local magistrate Hector Bridges, whose subsequent death by shooting brings matters to a head in dramatic fashion. Barnaby and Troy's investigation uncovers the victim has a history with one of the travellers - but revelations that he terrorised his family suggest that the killer may be closer to home

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The detective and his team embark on a race against time to recapture a dangerous convict who has escaped from Bodmin prison - a case that leads them to an elaborate drugs racket. Starring Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters, Jimmy Yuill and Sean Chapman

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Gerald Hadleigh, the secretary of the local writers' circle in the tranquil village of Midsomer Worthy, is found battered to death on the eve of a visit by a best-selling novelist. Barnaby and Troy discover Hadleigh was a mystery man, with no relatives or marriage certificate to match the wedding photo on display in his house. To make matters worse, another body is then found. Mystery, guest starring Anna Massey and Una Stubbs

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As the Second World War continues to rage, Foyle investigates a pro-Hitler organisation he suspects may be linked to a mysterious death at the White Feather Hotel, while reluctant assistant Milner struggles to decide where his loyalties lie. Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and Anthony Howell, with Charles Dance and Maggie Steed

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster is invited to the wedding of her former housemaid Gwenda, only to be caught up in a murder mystery involving the groom's family. Geraldine McEwan stars, with Juliet Stevenson, Denis Lawson and Alison Steadman

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Mike and Sally plan a holiday, but things run far from smoothly when Sid gets involved with the financial planning. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The Ropers take in a lodger, and Mildred is delighted when he begins doing all the household jobs that George will not tackle. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As the Second World War continues to rage, Foyle investigates a pro-Hitler organisation he suspects may be linked to a mysterious death at the White Feather Hotel, while reluctant assistant Milner struggles to decide where his loyalties lie. Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and Anthony Howell, with Charles Dance and Maggie Steed

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The plight of two young girls is brought to the attention of Carol, who discovers them home alone. Joe confronts their parents who are out drinking, but finds stepfather Brian unremorseful and reluctant to leave the pub. Gina is distracted from her worries about her unborn baby by preparations for the imminent arrival of Phil's niece Dawn, but when David goes to meet the girl from the station she is nowhere to be seen. Nikki Sanderson joins the cast

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Steve is determined to go to work despite being beaten up. Roy gives Kelly some home truths. Kathy is sure Eric has plans to ruin her

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy struggles to cope with breakfasts at the tearooms. Kim receives the vet's bill for Kaiser. Heather pours her heart out to Terry

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gary tells Judy they cannot buy Zoe's baby as it is illegal. Les plans to make a pass at Samantha. Jim tells Fiona he cannot get her out of his mind

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alan is stunned when Fiona suggests they get married as soon as possible. Zoe lets Audrey jump to the conclusion that Gary is the father of her baby

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A fox-hunt turns sour when a tramp is found dead in the woods, and matters are further complicated by the murder of the chief suspect's father. As DCI Barnaby investigates, he concludes it is only a matter of time before another member of the tight-knit community suffers the same fate. Guest starring James Bolam

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Aircraft manufacturer Lord Mayfield hosts a party to trap a woman suspected of being a spy for the Germans. Fearing events might not go according to plan, Lady Mayfield decides to summon the famous Belgian detective to keep an eye on proceedings. Starring David Suchet, with John Stride and Phyllida Law

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The detective applies his professional know-how to a fatal shooting, and soon begins to suspect that the dead man's brother-in-law may know more about the case than he is letting on - a theory borne out by forensic tests on the murder weapon. Jack Shepherd stars as the dour detective, with Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A young woman asks Poirot to investigate her father's murder - a 14-year-old crime for which her mother was found guilty and executed. The detective agrees to investigate, but warns his client that he may discover things she does not want to hear. David Suchet stars, with Rachael Stirling, Aidan Gillen, Aimee Mullens, Toby Stephens, Gemma Jones, Patrick Malahide and Annette Badland

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The unassuming detective launches an investigation when a conscientious objector dies while in police custody, and the judge who ordered his arrest is nearly killed in his booby-trapped home. His inquiries uncover a web of lies and corruption in the heart of wartime Hastings. Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and David Tennant

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A gathering at the Devon estate of Miss Marple's old school friend Lady Tressilian leads to murder when the eccentric Mr Treves tells the story of an unconvicted child killer. Miss Marple is on hand to assist Supt Mallard of the local constabulary, but not before a second victim is claimed. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, with guest appearances by Eileen Atkins, Tom Baker, Paul Nicholls, Alan Davies and Greg Wise, with a cameo by former British number-one tennis player Greg Rusedski

programme.desc: Omnibus. Liv is diagnosed with epilepsy, Harriet tries to get Charles off her back, and Will gets a job

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin sleeps on Bill's floor. Sally is cold towards Rita for not warning her about Kevin and Natalie. Roy demands his money back from Les

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin is stunned when Natalie tells him Sally slapped her. Percy starts to keep a record of the Battersbys' anti-social behaviour. Natalie pours out her heart to Audrey

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris isn't happy about the Dingles being at Home Farm. Zoe tells Kelly she is welcome to stay with her. Zak gets a shock when he opens the boot of Lisa's classic car

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Terry encourages Will to think more seriously about playing rugby. Butch and Zak argue about who is going to bury the body. Donna and Kirsty decide to get their noses pierced

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Anti-fur protester Sue Padgett finds herself in deep trouble when her boyfriend and fellow crusader Nick Payton starts a fire at a haulier's depot. Carol and Chris fall out when they offer opposing advice to a pregnant teenager, while Peggy enthusiastically prepares David for a date with a farmer's ample daughter Tina Bestwick. David Roper and Amie Titterton guest star

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid dismisses Jean's interest in the daily horoscopes as complete nonsense - but his opinion changes when they start coming true

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George seeks solace when his budgie dies, but Mildred is too preoccupied with finding something good enough to donate to the jumble sale. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster teams up with a dashing man to investigate a series of poison-pen letters in the sleepy village of Lymstock. Two of the missives have led to the deaths of Mona Symmington, the wife of Lymstock solicitor Richard, and Agnes Woddell, the parlour maid at the Symmingtons' house. However, Miss Marple suspects there is more to these deaths than meets the eye. Murder mystery with an all-star cast, featuring Geraldine McEwan, James D'Arcy, Emilia Fox, Imogen Stubbs, Ken Russell, Harry Enfield and Kelly Brook

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: When teenager Vivienne Middleton is reported missing Joe suspects her disappearance has something to do with her American boyfriend Bobby, and sets out to locate him. Meanwhile, David and Peggy's latest exploit, to use Greengrass's old metal detector to find artefacts about the village, puts them under suspicion when priceless bronze statues are stolen from local estates. Guest starring Adrian Lukis and Tessa Peake-Jones

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Butch falls asleep while guarding the body. Micky Bell gives Steve a hard time at the quarry. Chris scolds the Dingles for making a mess of the nursery flat

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim is annoyed at Steve's behaviour the previous night. The headmaster excludes Donna and Kirsty from school. A fire breaks out at the Hutchinsons' house

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Janice orders Leanne to find a job. Ashley is upset to hear about Maxine being with Steve. Alan accuses Fiona of still being in love with Steve and breaks their engagement

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly has a black eye and has to buy new glasses. Fiona puts a brave face on her bust-up with Alan. Natalie is thrilled when Kevin tells her he has decided he wants to be with her

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Troy is playing in an annual cricket match between Midsomer Worthy and Fletchers Cross when tragedy strikes. The wife of one of the team captains, a wealthy local landowner, is found battered to death with a cricket bat in a quarry. As Barnaby and Troy investigate, they discover no end of people with a grudge against the victim's husband - who may have been complicit in some other suspicious deaths. Guest starring Imelda Staunton and Robert Hardy

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth takes a break on the island of Rhodes, where an English beauty draws attention by flirting with fellow guests at the Palace Hotel. When she is found murdered, her husband is immediately suspected, but Poirot quickly deduces there is more to the case than a simple crime of passion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: When a wealthy businessman is killed in a mysterious explosion aboard his fishing boat and suspicion falls on his son, Wycliffe feels sure the real culprit is to be found elsewhere and unearths several locals with a motive for murder. Jack Shepherd stars as the Cornish copper, with Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. His inquiries uncover a web of lies and corruption in the heart of wartime Hastings. Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and David Tennant

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A gathering at the Devon estate of Miss Marple's old school friend Lady Tressilian leads to murder when the eccentric Mr Treves tells the story of an unconvicted child killer. Miss Marple is on hand to assist Supt Mallard of the local constabulary, but not before a second victim is claimed. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, with guest appearances by Eileen Atkins, Tom Baker, Paul Nicholls, Alan Davies and Greg Wise, with a cameo by former British number-one tennis player Greg Rusedski

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: The team are called in to help a KGB agent find a defecting Russian scientist who has disappeared with a deadly toxin. Drama, starring Robert Vaughn, Nyree Dawn Porter and Tony Anholt

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A desperate girl contacts Holmes and asks him to clear her boyfriend of killing his father, a farmer found bludgeoned to death beside a lake. Period drama, starring Jeremy Brett , Peter Vaughan and James Purefoy

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: An attempt on her colleague's life scares Makepeace and she threatens to resign, but she may be hiding her true feelings. Detective drama, starring Glynis Barber and Michael Brandon

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan is forced to impose himself on an old girlfriend whose flat is conveniently near a bank that intelligence has revealed is to be raided. Once in position, he and Carter try to pinpoint a radio transmitter that is being used to coordinate the movements of the robbers. Detective drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Two spies obtain information about a secret missile base intending to pass it to the other side via a person known as Captain Caruso, whom Steed and Mrs Peel are tasked to locate. Vintage adventure, with Patrick Macnee and Diana Rigg

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: An ex-footballer persuades Arthur to buy 2,000 tickets for an England v Scotland football match. However, trouble ensues once it becomes apparent they are forgeries. George Cole and Dennis Waterman star

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle teams up with a Hong Kong cop, who is in London trailing a Triad gang supplying drugs to US army personnel in Germany while operating an international protection racket. Meanwhile, Bodie goes undercover as a down-and-out in a squat where two young drug addicts have died. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A governess is accused of shooting dead her millionaire employer's wife on a remote bridge - but Holmes' deductive powers suggest an alternative explanation to the mystery. Period detective drama, guest starring Daniel Massey, with Jeremy Brett and Edward Hardwicke

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: The unlikely duo investigate a case of caviar smuggling that led to murder. Feature-length episode of the detective thriller, starring Glynis Barber and Michael Brandon

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed and Ms Peel investigate the case of several businessman who have been killed by birds of prey - and discover a connection to a comic-book character

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A wealthy family insists Dr Sudbury, a hypnotist friend of Arthur's, has mesmerised its teenage daughter Rita into agreeing to marry him. Arthur finds himself immersed in the fracas, while simultaneously trying to take over the affairs of a steam-cleaning business. Light-hearted drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 investigates a consortium building a lethal fighter plane in eastern Europe, that appears willing to kill to supress publication of the aircraft's appalling safetly record. Lynda Bellingham guest stars, with Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Emmerdale's Natalie Anderson (Alicia Metcalfe), actress Patsy Kensit, broadcaster Esther Rantzen and singer-presenter Brian McFadden compete to win thousands of pounds for charity by taking on one of the show's resident trivia experts in a test of their general knowledge. However, they must work as a team to build their prize pot and stay one step ahead of the Chaser. Bradley Walsh hosts

programme.title: Extreme Salvage Squad
programme.desc: The world of marine salvage and rescue in Australia, filmed in the Whitsunday Islands off Queensland

programme.title: From Russia with Love (1963)
programme.desc: James Bond is sent to steal a top-secret Soviet decoding machine and ends up helping its operator defect to the West. However, he discovers the mission is really a trap set by international crime syndicate Spectre, and struggles to evade his would-be assassins. Spy thriller, starring Sean Connery, Robert Shaw, Daniela Bianchi and Lotte Lenya

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed and Emma visit the countryside to investigate rumours of a ghost seen in the private chapel of a long-dead duke supposedly killed in a mining disaster. Julian Glover guest stars

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur's car lot is threatened with closure, spurring him to enter the cut-and-thrust world of local politics to have more of a say in local issues. Comedy drama, starring Dennis Waterman, George Cole and Lennard Pearce, best remembered as Grandad in Only Fools and Horses

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are trained to act as bodyguards to a Middle Eastern president who is preparing to sign an important peace treaty. Meanwhile, two killers are receiving similar training to prepare them to assassinate him. As their paths cross, the CI5 operatives learn the would-be killers have some useful inside information and share more in common with them than they may have imagined

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Radio 2 presenter Ken Bruce, former England midfielder Paul Ince, EastEnders actress Laila Morse (Mo Harris) and boxer David Haye compete to win thousands of pounds for charity by taking on one of the show's resident trivia experts in a test of their general knowledge. programme.title: Goldfinger (1964)
programme.desc: James Bond is assigned to investigate a notorious German gold smuggler and discovers his quarry has ambitions much greater than first suspected. Spy adventure, starring Sean Connery, Gert Frobe and Honor Blackman

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: An antique necklace and a conspiracy of desperate men drag the duo into the shady world of gambling. Police drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan and Carter uncover a plot to smuggle gold bars into the Middle East disguised as weights for an international strongman competition. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: A man-eating tiger is on the loose - but the big cat attack is just the first step in a plot to take over the country. Adventure, starring Patrick Macnee and Diana Rigg, with Ronnie Barker

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is conned into buying a dodgy greyhound and lumbered with a pile of knocked-off videos, and it is up to Terry to save him from a fate worse than death. Vintage drama, starring Dennis Waterman, George Cole and Patrick Malahide

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle's country holiday with their latest girlfriends spirals into chaos when they are held hostage in a farmhouse by two gunmen who have just botched a robbery. Adventure, with Gordon Jackson, Lewis Collins and Martin Shaw

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade embarks on one of his most challenging journeys as he investigates reports of a face-eating creature in a remote part of Bolivia. Finding his skills and knowledge tested to the limit, he encounters one of his oldest adversaries and discovers how a species can adapt its behaviour to survive

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Ali Hamidi, Neil Spooner and Tom Dove travel to Thailand, where they have previously caught huge Siamese carp, but this time the focus is on the other species of carp found there. Their adventure is split between Fishing Park Samui and Gillhams Fishing Resorts, where they will have to use all of their skill and knowledge to outwit as many different carp species as possible, and in the process they will be hoping to bank one of the stunning Jullien's golden carp that can be found there

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: Skipper Squizzy and Tabor are halfway through their five-day run. They have just 48 hours to hit their target - and then sell 500 kilos of lobsters direct to punters

programme.title: Thunderball (1965)
programme.desc: James Bond travels to the Bahamas on the trail of two nuclear warheads stolen from a British aircraft. The lethal weapons have fallen into the hands of international crime syndicate Spectre, which plans to fire them at the US coast unless an exorbitant ransom is paid. Spy adventure, starring Sean Connery, Claudine Auger, Adolfo Celi and Lois Maxwell
